steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The archaeologists' assumption is: a major demographic shift causes simultaneous reductions in multiple specialized crafting traditions. The evidence shows that around 1500 BCE, gold filigree declined steeply—but advanced copper-alloy metallurgy and most other specialized traditions continued to flourish. The blank must draw an inference consistent with both the assumption and the evidence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'disruptions around 1500 BCE in the utilization of gold filigree likely occurred in the context of demographic continuity': only one tradition declined while others flourished. Under the assumption, a demographic shift would have caused simultaneous declines in multiple traditions—so the absence of that pattern points to demographic continuity. ✓
  • Choice B — 'a sudden simultaneous decline in artifacts from multiple specialized crafting traditions is less likely … to indicate demographic changes': this directly contradicts the assumption stated in the passage, which holds that simultaneous declines do indicate demographic changes. ✗
  • Choice C — 'peoples in the Alazani River valley … uphold multiple specialized crafting traditions, including goldsmithing and copper-alloy metallurgy, even as demographic shifts occurred': the evidence shows gold filigree declined, so goldsmithing was not upheld. Also, asserting demographic shifts occurred contradicts what the evidence implies under the assumption. ✗
  • Choice D — 'cross-cultural transmission between distinct demographic groups … likely explains the expansion of copper-alloy metallurgy': the passage does not mention expansion of copper-alloy metallurgy, only that it continued. This introduces an explanation not supported by the data. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
